chiefComplaints: Heavy menstrual bleeding (menorrhagia) for the past 8 months, lower abdominal pain, and pressure symptoms.
hpi: 46-year-old female presenting with a history of menorrhagia, passing clots during menstruation, and dull-aching lower abdominal pain. Symptoms have progressively worsened over the last 3 months, causing fatigue and exertional dyspnea. Ultrasound abdomen-pelvis shows a large intramural fibroid measuring 6.2 x 5.8 cm in the anterior wall of the uterus and multiple smaller subserosal fibroids.
relevantClinicalFindings: Uterus enlarged to 14 weeks size, firm, mobile. Severe pallor present. Hb: 8.4 g/dL. Pelvic USG: Multiple uterine fibroids (largest intramural 62mm). Endometrial thickness: 9mm. Patient is planned for Total Laparoscopic Hysterectomy (TLH) with Bilateral Salpingo-Oophorectomy (BSO).
additionalClinicalNotes: Patient has a history of primary hypertension controlled on Tab. Amlodipine 5mg OD. No history of previous abdominal surgeries.
treatmentTakenSoFar: Oral Hemostatics (Tranexamic acid) and NSAIDs with temporary relief.
reasonForHospitalisation: Surgical management of symptomatic uterine fibroids (TLH + BSO).
notes: This case tests the TPA billing validator for a multi-procedure gynecological case. The hospital unbundled TLH, BSO, and Adhesiolysis as separate packages, whereas BSO is considered inclusive or subject to a 50% discount. Additionally, the patient upgraded to a Deluxe Suite (6,000/day) despite being eligible only for a Single Private A/C Room (capped at 3,000/day), triggering a proportional deduction across all non-consumable associate expenses.
realGap: Unbundling of surgical packages (charging BSO and Adhesiolysis separately from TLH) and breach of room rent capping resulting in proportional deductions on associated medical charges under GIPSA guidelines.
sourceReasoning: Under GIPSA/PPN guidelines and Star Health policy terms, if the patient opts for a room category higher than eligibility, proportional deductions apply to all associated expenses (OT, Surgeon fee, Anaesthetist charges, Nursing, etc.). Furthermore, secondary surgeries performed through the same incision/portal during the same session must be bundled or discounted by 50% rather than billed as independent full packages.
rawDocumentText:
DISCHARGE SUMMARY - APEX MULTISPECIALITY HOSPITAL, DELHI

PATIENT NAME: Mrs. Sunita Sharma  | AGE: 46 Years | GENDER: Female
IPD NO: IP-887361-24 | ADMISSION DATE: 12-Oct-2024 | DISCHARGE DATE: 16-Oct-2024
ATTENDING CONSULTANT: Dr. Meenakshi Iyer, MD, DGO (Gynecology)
INSURANCE POLICY NO: SH/2024/1002938 | INSURER: Star Health and Allied Insurance

DIAGNOSIS:
1. Multiple Uterine Fibroids (D25.9)
2. Menorrhagia with Secondary Anemia
3. Essential Hypertension (I10)

PROCEDURE PERFORMED:
Total Laparoscopic Hysterectomy (TLH) + Bilateral Salpingo-Oophorectomy (BSO) + Extensive Adhesiolysis under General Anesthesia on 13-Oct-2024.

CLINICAL SUMMARY:
Patient presented with severe menorrhagia since 8 months, with dysmenorrhea and pressure symptoms over the bladder. Not controlled on conservative medical therapy. Blood transfusion of 1 unit PCV was given pre-operatively on 12-Oct-2024 to correct anemia (pre-op Hb 8.4 g/dL, post-op Hb 10.2 g/dL). Intraoperatively, dense omental and bowel adhesions were noted on the posterior uterine wall, which were lysed. TLH + BSO was completed uneventfully. Specimen sent for histopathology.

ROOM DETAILS OCCUPIED:
Deluxe Suite (Room No 402) - Charged @ INR 6,000/- per day. (Note: Policy eligible room is Single Private A/C Room, capped at INR 3,000/- per day or 1% of SI).

ITEMIZED HOSPITAL BILL SUMMARY:
1. Deluxe Suite Room Rent (4 Days @ INR 6000/day): INR 24,000
2. TLH Surgery Package Charge (Primary): INR 1,20,000
3. Bilateral Salpingo-Oophorectomy Package Charge (Secondary): INR 45,000
4. Pelvic Adhesiolysis Surcharge: INR 30,000
5. Surgeon Fees (Specialist): INR 20,000
6. Operation Theatre (OT) Charges: INR 15,000
7. Anesthesia Charges: INR 10,000
8. Consumables & Pharmacy: INR 21,000
--------------------------------------------
TOTAL BILLED AMOUNT: INR 2,85,000
--------------------------------------------

Dr. Meenakshi Iyer, Attending Gynecologist
Reg No: DMC/G/442938
simulatedDenialReason: Authorization partially approved. The TPA has disallowed the separate package charges for Bilateral Salpingo-Oophorectomy (INR 45,000) and Adhesiolysis (INR 30,000) as they are considered integral parts of the primary procedure (TLH). Furthermore, proportional deduction of 50% has been applied to OT charges, Surgeon fees, and Nursing charges due to the patient occupying a Deluxe Suite room (INR 6,000/day) against her eligible category of Single Private A/C room (INR 3,000/day).
